name: Basic Divinity
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the thermometer by placing it in a small saucepan of cold water and bringing it to a boil. Let simmer until needed.
2. Separate eggs: place whites in a large bowl and yolks back into refrigerator for another use.
3. Fill a glass with ice cubes and water.
4. Measure sugar, corn syrup, ice water, and salt into a heavy 2-quart saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
5. Dissolve the sugar by stirring constantly over low heat until clear, gritty sounds stop, and spoon glides smoothly.
6. Increase heat to medium and bring to a boil.
7. (Optional): Cover pan with wax paper and lid, steaming for 2-3 minutes to dissolve sugar crystals without boiling over.
8. Wash any remaining crystals from the sides with hot water from the thermometer bath.
9. Introduce the prewarmed thermometer into the mixture.
10. Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry; beat if mixer is heavy-duty and bubbles large before adding.
11. Test syrup at 240 Degrees F (115.5 C) then continue to test until it reaches firm-ball stage of 246-260 Degrees F (119-126.5 C).
12. Syrup should be hard to scrape in ice water, force into a ball that holds shape but gives under pressure.
13. Return thermometer to hot water bath for cleaning.
14. Dribble syrup into egg whites slowly and steadily while beating at low speed; tilt pan to incorporate last drops without scraping pan.
15. Switch to flat whip if available once fully incorporated.
16. Beat until highest mixer speed is reached, considering machine power and humidity.
17. Test divinity: Lift beaters; it should not fall back in ribbons but form a stationary column between beaters and bowl.
18. Candy will lose sheen, become less sticky, and drop onto wax paper holding shape without peak formation.
19. If needed, place candy in freezer until ready or continue by hand.
20. Fold in flavorings and nuts/nachos using mixer; if too thick with spoon.
21. Drop or spread divinity on wax paper-covered cookie sheets or buttered pan.
22. Refrigerate until set (24 hours recommended). Cut into squares.
23. Store in airtight container at room temperature or refrigerator.
